name: Social Problems: Population, Education, Health, Employment, Poverty (with TN Human Development Indicators)

sourceRefs.label: 2026_q page 54 Q55 (unemployment as a social problem — Assertion-Reason, with answer key)
2025_q page 16 Q14-15 (Kerala's highest unemployment 2022-23; TN's 47% higher-education GER 2021-22, with answer key — time-sensitive)
2026_q page 134 Q160 (TN district literacy-rate ranking, 2011 census: Kanyakumari/Chennai/Thoothukudi/Nilgiris, with answer key)
tnpscportal.in/spc.tn.gov.in - TN's Human Development Index ranking, health/literacy indicators
NITI Aayog's Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) — health/education/standard-of-living dimensions

researchNotes: 4 real hits, 2 explicitly time-sensitive (2022-23 unemployment rate, 2021-22 GER figure) and flagged as such rather than presented as permanent facts. The literacy-ranking fact (2011 census) is more durable since it's tied to a specific historical census, not a recurring annual figure. Round-2 pass (2026-07, second expansion): added the Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) as a standard, stable concept complementing the more time-sensitive unemployment/GER figures already in this topic.

reading.intro: Covers unemployment and poverty as social problems, and Tamil Nadu's standing on Human Development Indicators (literacy, health, employment) compared to the rest of India. Several facts here are explicitly time-bound (tied to a specific year's data) — flagged so they aren't mistaken for permanent facts.

qa.question: Real exam (Assertion-Reason): Is unemployment a major social problem, and does financial-aid provision directly EXPLAIN why it causes sickness/disorganization?
qa.answer: Both statements are TRUE — unemployment IS a major social problem causing sickness and personal/family/community disorganization, AND social provisions for financial aid do alleviate the suffering unemployment entails — but a real exam confirmed the second statement is NOT the correct EXPLANATION of the first; they're both true facts that don't have that specific cause-effect relationship to each other.

qa.question: Real exam: Which state had the highest unemployment percentage during 2022-23 (among Kerala, Haryana, Punjab, Tamil Nadu)?
qa.answer: Kerala — confirmed by a real exam question. (Note: this is a specific year's snapshot, not a permanent ranking — unemployment rates fluctuate yearly.)

qa.question: Real exam: What was Tamil Nadu's Gross Enrollment Ratio (GER) in higher education during 2021-2022?
qa.answer: 47 percent — confirmed by a real exam question. (Also a specific year's figure, useful as a benchmark but expected to change in later editions of the same statistic.)

qa.question: Real exam: As per the 2011 census, how do The Nilgiris, Chennai, Thoothukudi, and Kanyakumari rank by literacy rate (highest to lowest)?
qa.answer: Kanyakumari (highest), then Chennai, then Thoothukudi, then The Nilgiris (lowest among these four) — confirmed by a real exam question. Kanyakumari is generally known to have the highest literacy rate among all Tamil Nadu districts.

qa.question: How does Tamil Nadu generally compare to other Indian states on the Human Development Index (HDI) and related indicators?
qa.answer: Tamil Nadu typically ranks among India's top 5 states on HDI (rankings vary by year/source, roughly 3rd-5th), behind Kerala (which consistently ranks 1st nationally on HDI). TN performs particularly well on health indicators (lower infant/child mortality than the national average) and is among the top 3 states by per-capita income and GDP contribution.

qa.question: What is the difference between 'unemployment' and 'underemployment' as distinct social problems?
qa.answer: Unemployment means having no work at all despite actively seeking it. Underemployment means having work, but in a job below one's skill level, or for fewer hours than desired — both are counted differently in labour statistics and represent distinct policy challenges.

qa.question: What is the National Family Health Survey (NFHS), and why does it matter for tracking these social indicators?
qa.answer: A large-scale, periodic government survey (5 rounds conducted so far) tracking health, nutrition, and population indicators across India and its states — the primary data source policymakers use to assess health/social progress at the state level, including many of Tamil Nadu's HDI-related indicators.

qa.question: Round-2 pass — What is the Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI), and how does it differ from a simple income-based poverty measure?
qa.answer: Released by NITI Aayog for India (based on a global UNDP/Oxford methodology), it measures poverty across 3 dimensions — health, education, and standard of living — using 12 indicators, rather than just income/consumption levels. This captures deprivation more holistically than a purely monetary poverty line.

questions.prompt: Assertion [A]: Unemployment is a major social problem causing sickness and family/community disorganization. Reason [R]: Social financial-aid provisions alleviate the suffering unemployment causes. Choose the correct option.
options: Both [A] and [R] are true, and [R] is the correct explanation of [A]
Both [A] and [R] are true, but [R] is NOT the correct explanation of [A]
questions.explanation: Both true, but [R] does not explain [A].

questions.prompt: Which state had the highest unemployment percentage during 2022-23, among Kerala, Haryana, Punjab, and Tamil Nadu?
Tamil Nadu's Gross Enrollment Ratio (GER) in higher education during 2021-2022 was:
As per the 2011 census, which Tamil Nadu district among the following had the HIGHEST literacy rate?
As per the 2011 census, arrange these districts by literacy rate from highest to lowest: The Nilgiris, Chennai, Thoothukudi, Kanyakumari.
Which state consistently ranks 1st nationally on the Human Development Index (HDI)?
On health indicators (e.g., infant/child mortality), how does Tamil Nadu generally compare to the national average?
The key difference between unemployment and underemployment is:
options: They mean exactly the same thing
Unemployment = no work at all; underemployment = working below one's skill/hours desired
questions.explanation: Unemployment = no work; underemployment = working below capacity/desired hours.

questions.prompt: The National Family Health Survey (NFHS) is primarily used to track:
NITI Aayog's Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) measures poverty across which three dimensions?
options: Health, education, and standard of living
questions.explanation: Health, education, and standard of living — using 12 underlying indicators.
